title: Micron Stock Is Up 70% in 2026. Here's Why Analysts Still See More Upside Ahead
text: Analysts remain bullish on Micron despite the stock's 70% year-to-date rally for several key reasons. The company's Q2 fiscal 2026 results demonstrated exceptional performance, with adjusted EPS came in at $12.20, beating the consensus of $9.21 by 32.7% and revenue substantially exceeding expectations. High-bandwidth memory represents a structural opportunity with the company sold out of HBM for the next several quarters, indicating robust demand from AI processor manufacturers with limited near-term supply. The valuation model targets $664 per share, suggesting 22.5% total upside from the current share price of $542 over 2.3 years, implying continued appreciation potential. Management maintains that AI memory demand constitutes a multi-year structural shift, not a single cycle, suggesting this growth phase extends beyond temporary cyclicality. Being added to the S&P 100 and receiving fresh analyst coverage (D.A. Davidson's Buy initiation) provides institutional support and validation.
